meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client builds. Master SaaS pricing strategies and use Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your projects faster and hit your revenue goals.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is often scope creep disguised as 'over-engineering.' By implementing tiered SaaS pricing, you force a minimum viable product scope. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, ensuring you ship functional, billable software in days, not months.
content.intro:
The blinking cursor is the graveyard of ambition. You’ve been here before: a promising client project, a surge of initial momentum, and then the slow, agonizing descent into feature creep and technical debt. You aren't failing because you lack skill; you are failing because you lack a rigid delivery framework that ties your code to a concrete pricing model.

It is time to stop acting like a hobbyist and start operating like a factory. When you define your SaaS pricing tiers before writing a single line of code, you build boundaries. By leveraging the combined power of Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform from a struggling developer into a high-velocity ship-machine that delivers value on time, every time.

steps.title: Define Tiered Value Proposition
steps.description: Map out your SaaS tiers to limit initial scope. We focus on the 'Core' tier to ensure the project is shippable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a feature breakdown for a 3-tier SaaS pricing model. Focus on a 'Core' tier that is achievable in 10 days of development.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project roadmap based on the 3-tier pricing strategy defined in Antigravity.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate, ensuring you don't waste time on repetitive setup tasks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern SaaS boilerplate using Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase. Include authentication and basic billing integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project scaffolding generated by Antigravity and verify environment variables.'

steps.title: Implement Pricing Logic
steps.description: Integrate Stripe or a similar payment gateway using Claude Code to handle the backend logic for your pricing tiers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the Stripe webhook handler logic for a 3-tier subscription system using TypeScript.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Inject Stripe webhook handlers into the backend and connect to user subscription status.'

steps.title: Automate Feature Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build out specific features based on the pricing tier requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular component strategy for a SaaS dashboard where features unlock based on the user's subscription tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement role-based UI component rendering based on subscription level in the dashboard.'

steps.title: Final Deployment and Validation
steps.description: Run your final test suite to ensure the pricing model is correctly preventing unauthorized access to premium features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test suite for verifying that Free tier users cannot access Pro tier API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute automated tests and deploy to production environment.'

pro_tips: Always price your 'Enterprise' tier at 3x your 'Pro' tier to anchor the value of your middle offering.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation for your client as you code; it saves hours of post-project admin.
If a feature request doesn't fit into a pre-defined pricing tier, charge it as an 'add-on' to protect your timeline.
Keep your database schema flexible so you can add new tiers without rewriting your entire backend.
Automate your deployment pipeline so that every commit is a potential release candidate, keeping your client updated in real-time.
content.next_steps: Once your first tier is live, focus on gathering user feedback for 14 days before adding the secondary features. Don't build for the sake of building; build for the sake of selling.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting unfinished projects sit on your hard drive. By aligning your pricing with your technical constraints, you gain the clarity needed to ship. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
